scope.summary:
OPERATOR + GLOBAL-BUILD-MANUAL: update the internal manual so a FUTURE NO-CONTEXT MODEL can use the now-usable product. The notes+chat worksurface (MT-097/098), the working open/edit/save notes flow (MT-099), the terminal + local/cloud model launch in a repo folder (MT-100/101), the Settings->Diagnostics panel (MT-087), and the visual debugger + foreground-safe navigation (MT-102/103) all landed — the built-in manual must now teach them.

VERIFIED STATE (live-app inspection 2026-06-27): the internal manual EXISTS and is the reuse surface — this is a CONTENT UPDATE to the existing manual, not a new subsystem:
- src/frontend/handshake_native/src/manual_pane.rs (MT-073): the ManualRegistry / ManualSection / ManualTopic data model + the ManualPane widget + the AgentToolReference (the author_id -> MCP tool steering index) + the AccessKit ids (manual-pane / manual-search / manual-topic-{section}.{topic}).
- src/frontend/handshake_native/src/manual_content_editors.rs (MT-073): editors_manual_section() returns the live ManualSection — the eight GLOBAL-BUILD-MANUAL topics (Purpose / Core Workflows / Startup and Run / Inputs and Outputs / Navigation Paths / Safety Constraints / Common Failure Modes / Recovery Steps), the Interop Edges topic, and the Agent Tool Reference (agent_tool_rows()). app.rs registers it: ManualRegistry::new().register_section(editors_manual_section()) (~line 896).
- The agent-tool reference uses the REAL author_ids (from the live registries) + the FOUR REAL MCP tool names (list_widgets / click_widget / set_value / screenshot) — NOT invented gui.* names (the MT-073 id-audit discipline).

What to update (extend the existing manual content; the manual pane is registered as a factory but stripped from the notes-only default per MT-097 — it stays REACHABLE via the manual surface / Settings, just not on the default worksurface):
- EDIT manual_content_editors.rs to ADD/UPDATE manual topics so a no-context model knows how to use: (1) THE NOTES WORKSURFACE + CHAT — that the default worksurface is the notes editors (code + rich) with the chat pane beside them (MT-097/098); the pane author_ids (code editor, rich/Notes editor 'loom.wikipage'-class, runtime-chat-panel/runtime-chat-input/runtime-chat-send); (2) OPENING/EDITING/SAVING NOTES — the open-by-id -> live edit -> authoritative save -> reopen-shows-edit flow (MT-099) + the save route + the draft-vs-save distinction + the EventLedger receipt; (3) LAUNCHING A TERMINAL in a repo folder with the wrapper (MT-100) — the 'Terminal: Open in Workspace Folder' command + the HONEST state (the PTY runtime exists but the native HTTP/IPC reach may be a typed blocker — state it honestly, do not claim a working terminal if MT-100 landed a blocker); (4) LAUNCHING A LOCAL/CLOUD MODEL in a repo folder with the wrapper (MT-101) — the 'Launch Model Session in Workspace Folder' command + the reachable POST /jobs half + the IPC-only typed-blocker half (honestly); (5) THE SETTINGS->DIAGNOSTICS PANEL (MT-087) — that diagnostics (heartbeat/frame/resource/last-N events + the Tier-3 Palmistry section) lives in Settings, NOT the worksurface; (6) THE VISUAL DEBUGGER (MT-102) — the WorksurfaceInspector dump (pane tree + widget-id inventory + layout tree + screenshot) in Settings/Diagnostics, with the headless-GPU pixel-readback known-limit; (7) FOREGROUND-SAFE NAVIGATION (MT-103) — that a model drives the GUI via the MCP click_widget/set_value/list_widgets path with NO OS foreground/input-injection (HBR-QUIET), targets by stable author_id.
- UPDATE the Agent Tool Reference rows (agent_tool_rows()) to ADD the new addressable controls (runtime-chat-input/send, the terminal/model launch commands, the visual-debugger/diagnostics surface ids) mapped to the REAL MCP tools (list_widgets/click_widget/set_value/screenshot) — every author_id MUST be a LIVE registered id (the MT-073 id-audit test cross-checks; an orphan id FAILS the build).
- HONESTY: where MT-100/101 landed a typed blocker (terminal native reach / IPC-only model spawn), the manual states the HONEST state (what works, what is a typed blocker), NOT a fabricated 'it works'. The manual reflects the ACTUAL shipped behavior of MT-097..103.
- EDIT/EXTEND tests/test_manual_content.rs (or the existing MT-073 manual content test) for the new topics + the id-audit over the new rows.

Reuse notes: the ManualRegistry/ManualSection/ManualTopic/AgentToolReference data model + the ManualPane widget + the id-audit are BUILT (manual_pane.rs / manual_content_editors.rs / MT-073). This MT adds CONTENT (topics + agent-tool rows) to the existing section. No new manual subsystem. src/backend untouched.

Risk if missed: a future no-context model cannot operate the now-usable product (notes+chat, terminal/model launch, diagnostics, visual debugger, safe navigation) — GLOBAL-BUILD-MANUAL unmet; OR the manual claims a feature works that actually landed a typed blocker (a dishonest manual).
forbidden_paths: src/frontend/handshake_native/src/mcp/** (reuse the real tool names via reference only)
acceptance_criteria: AC-104-1 (NEW MANUAL SECTIONS RENDER, prove first): the manual renders the new/updated topics covering — (1) the notes worksurface + chat, (2) opening/editing/saving notes, (3) launching a terminal in a repo folder, (4) launching a local/cloud model in a repo folder, (5) the Settings->Diagnostics panel, (6) the visual debugger, (7) foreground-safe navigation — proven by a kittest/widget test asserting each topic heading is present + selectable in the manual pane (the MT-073 heading-presence pattern) and the bodies are non-empty no-context prose (concrete commands/pane names/author_ids/keybinds).

AC-104-2 (AGENT-TOOL REFERENCE USES REAL IDs + REAL MCP TOOLS): the updated agent_tool_rows() add the new addressable controls (e.g. runtime-chat-input, runtime-chat-send, the terminal/model launch command ids, the visual-debugger/diagnostics surface ids) and each row's author_id is a LIVE registered id + each mcp_tool is one of the FOUR real tools (list_widgets/click_widget/set_value/screenshot) — proven by the id-audit test (an orphan author_id or an invented gui.* tool FAILS the build), exactly as MT-073 established.

AC-104-3 (HONEST STATE): where MT-100/101 landed a typed blocker, the manual states the HONEST state (what works vs what is a typed blocker) — proven by a test asserting the terminal/model topics name the typed-blocker condition (no fabricated 'fully working' claim) consistent with the MT-100/101 implementation_result.

AC-104-4 (DIAGNOSTICS IN SETTINGS, NOT WORKSURFACE): the manual states diagnostics + the visual debugger live in Settings->Diagnostics and the worksurface is notes+chat only — proven by a test asserting the diagnostics/visual-debugger topics point to the Settings surface (consistent with MT-087/097/102), not a worksurface feature pane.

AC-104-5: `cargo build -p handshake-native` (lib + bins) clean; clippy `--all-targets -D warnings` clean; full suite `-j 2` green (incl. the manual id-audit); external CARGO_TARGET_DIR; no Color32 literal outside theme/palette.rs (CONTROL-4); `git ls-files src/**/*.png` empty.

AC-104-6 (DIAGNOSTIC-TOOL TOPICS, one per tool): the manual renders a dedicated selectable topic for EACH of Flight Recorder, internal_diagnostics, and Palmistry (same depth as the editor-tool topics) with non-empty no-context bodies covering purpose/what-it-does/how-it-works/where-it-lives + the three-tier model (which tool catches which failure class) - proven by a kittest/widget test asserting all three diagnostic-tool topic headings are present + selectable with concrete no-context bodies, exactly as the editor-tool topics are.
proof_targets: PT-104-A: widget/kittest asserts the seven new/updated topic headings are present + selectable in the manual pane with non-empty no-context bodies.
PT-104-B: the manual id-audit test asserts every new agent_tool_rows() author_id is a LIVE registered id + every mcp_tool is one of the four real tools (orphan id / invented tool FAILS the build).
PT-104-C: test asserts the terminal/model topics state the honest typed-blocker condition (per MT-100/101) and the diagnostics/visual-debugger topics point to Settings (per MT-087/102).
PT-104-D: cargo build --lib --bins clean + clippy -D warnings clean + full suite -j 2 green (incl. id-audit) + CONTROL-4 green + git ls-files src/**/*.png empty.

PT-104-E: widget/kittest asserts the three diagnostic-tool topics (Flight Recorder, internal_diagnostics, Palmistry) are present + selectable in the manual with no-context bodies covering the three-tier model.
implementation_notes: OPERATOR STEER 2026-06-28 - DEDICATED MANUAL TOPIC PER DIAGNOSTIC TOOL: the internal manual MUST carry a standalone TOPIC for EACH of the three diagnostic tools, at the SAME depth + format as the editor-tool topics copied from the Obsidian/VS Code clones into Notes (Purpose / What it does / How it works / How to use it + where it lives / when to use it). Add a DIAGNOSTICS manual section (e.g. diagnostics_manual_section() registered in app.rs alongside editors_manual_section()) OR topics, with one topic each for: (1) FLIGHT RECORDER - Tier 1, the backend BUSINESS-EVENT ledger (what it records, that it is the canonical replay/audit log, where it surfaces, GET /events); kept-as-is, in handshake_core. (2) internal_diagnostics - Tier 2, Handshake-native IN-APP self-diagnostics (UI-thread heartbeat, frame-time, CPU/RSS/GPU, panic hook, the open diagnostic-event API, the StalledOperation watchdog MT-105); reachable in Settings->Diagnostics; how a model reads it. (3) PALMISTRY - Tier 3, the EXTERNAL out-of-process watcher that survives Handshake freezes/crashes/heavy-CPU (the shared-memory ring reader, freeze detection, crash->minidump, the durable survivor records, and the child-process stall watch MT-106); how its records surface (the Tier-3 section of the diagnostics panel). The three topics must explain the THREE-TIER model + when each catches a failure (FR=business events while healthy; internal_diagnostics=in-app health + stalls; Palmistry=freeze/crash survival when the app itself is dead). A no-context model must be able to pick the right diagnostic tool for a failure from these topics. Same id-audit discipline (every referenced author_id live, MCP tools = the four real names).
Use the EXTERNAL CARGO_TARGET_DIR (Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-native-target); NEVER a repo-local target/. Run the full suite with -j 2.

CONTENT UPDATE only: extend editors_manual_section() / agent_tool_rows() in manual_content_editors.rs (the existing MT-073 section). Reuse the ManualSection/ManualTopic/AgentToolReference data model + the ManualPane widget; do NOT build a new manual subsystem.

The agent-tool reference author_ids MUST be LIVE registered ids (the MT-073 id-audit cross-checks against the live AccessKit registries — an orphan id FAILS the build). Source the new ids from the real surfaces: runtime-chat-panel/input/send (MT-098), the terminal/model launch command ids (MT-100/101), the visual-debugger/diagnostics surface ids (MT-087/102). Use ONLY the four real MCP tool names (list_widgets/click_widget/set_value/screenshot) — never an invented gui.* name.

HONESTY: the manual must reflect the ACTUAL shipped behavior of MT-097..103. If MT-100 (terminal) or MT-101 (model spawn) landed a typed blocker (the IPC-only reach), the manual states what works (e.g. the reachable POST /jobs half for MT-101) and what is a typed blocker — do NOT claim a fully-working terminal/model launch if the implementation_result says blocker.

Diagnostics + the visual debugger are in Settings->Diagnostics (MT-087/102), and the worksurface is notes+chat only (MT-097) — the manual's Navigation Paths topic must reflect this (the manual pane itself is stripped from the notes-only default but stays reachable via the manual surface / Settings).
depends_on MT-097..103 (the manual documents what those MTs shipped); it is the closing MT of the U1 expansion (blocks: none).
Read the KERNEL_BUILDER coder protocol under .GOV/ first; the manual must be no-context-usable (concrete commands/pane names/author_ids/keybinds) and HONEST about typed blockers.
ports_from_react: NONE (native manual content update over the existing MT-073 manual subsystem).
binds_backend_api: NONE. The manual is in-process content; it documents (but does not call) the document/jobs routes. src/backend untouched. No SQLite.

hbr_int_009_tier_obligations.reason: The manual is in-process documentation content; it neither reads nor writes the Flight Recorder. FR stays as-is/forbidden. (MT-104 now also carries a DEDICATED manual TOPIC for this tier - documentation only, not a producer/consumer.)

The manual DOCUMENTS the Settings->Diagnostics panel + the visual debugger but emits no internal_diagnostics events itself; it is content, not a producer or consumer of the diagnostics ring. (MT-104 now also carries a DEDICATED manual TOPIC for this tier - documentation only, not a producer/consumer.)

The manual content does not produce or read freeze/crash/debris records; it describes where they surface (the MT-093 Palmistry section of the diagnostics panel). (MT-104 now also carries a DEDICATED manual TOPIC for this tier - documentation only, not a producer/consumer.)

handoff.coder_session: HISTORY 2026-07-01T04:08:49Z: manual honesty regression fixed in src/frontend/handshake_native/src/manual_content_editors.rs; model-session manual explicitly says the request session_id is correlation only and must not be fabricated as runtime proof. REVALIDATED 2026-07-01 by Codex: cargo test --manifest-path src/frontend/handshake_native/Cargo.toml --target-dir ../Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-native-target --test test_manual_content -- --nocapture => 7 passed, 0 failed. Settings proof initially exposed a current kittest harness regression: test_settings_dialog used Harness::run() against a live Settings surface with a blinking text cursor/repaint cause, so the normal suite could hit Harness::run max_steps. Root-cause fix: src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_settings_dialog.rs now uses bounded run_steps(3) pumps instead of idle-waiting run() calls. Fresh proof: cargo test --manifest-path src/frontend/handshake_native/Cargo.toml --target-dir ../Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-native-target --test test_settings_dialog -- --nocapture --test-threads=1 => 10 passed, 0 failed; cargo test --manifest-path src/frontend/handshake_native/Cargo.toml --target-dir ../Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-native-target --test test_settings_dialog -- --nocapture => 10 passed, 0 failed. MT-104 remains READY_FOR_VALIDATION pending WP validator review.

risks: RISK-104-1: a documented author_id is an orphan (not a live registered id), so a model addresses a non-existent control. Mitigate: AC-104-2 id-audit FAILS the build on any orphan id (the MT-073 discipline).

RISK-104-2: the manual claims the terminal/model launch fully works when MT-100/101 landed a typed blocker (a dishonest manual). Mitigate: AC-104-3 asserts the honest typed-blocker state consistent with the MT-100/101 implementation_result.
RISK-104-3: the manual documents an invented gui.* MCP tool instead of the four real tools. Mitigate: AC-104-2 asserts every mcp_tool is one of list_widgets/click_widget/set_value/screenshot.
RISK-104-4: the manual says diagnostics/visual-debugger live on the worksurface (contradicting MT-087/097/102). Mitigate: AC-104-4 asserts they point to Settings->Diagnostics.
RISK-104-5: a topic body is empty/hand-wavy (not no-context-usable). Mitigate: AC-104-1 asserts non-empty no-context bodies (concrete commands/pane names/author_ids/keybinds).
minimum_controls: MC-104-1: AC-104-1 renders the seven new/updated no-context topics.
MC-104-2: AC-104-2 id-audit keeps every author_id live + every mcp_tool real (orphan/invented FAILS the build).
MC-104-3: AC-104-3/4 keep the manual HONEST (typed-blocker state for terminal/model; diagnostics in Settings).
MC-104-4: external target + no tracked PNG + CONTROL-4 + full suite -j 2 green (AC-104-5).

research_provenance.source: WP-KERNEL-012 product-vision steer + GLOBAL-BUILD-MANUAL; live-app inspection (the MT-073 manual subsystem — manual_pane.rs + manual_content_editors.rs + the agent-tool id-audit — verified PRESENT; the four real MCP tool names verified).
research_provenance.field_stack: Built-in, in-app, no-context model manual (the GLOBAL-BUILD-MANUAL requirement): topics + an author_id -> MCP tool steering index, id-audited against the live registries. Reuses the MT-073 ManualRegistry/ManualSection/ManualTopic/AgentToolReference data model + the ManualPane widget + the id-audit test. No new subsystem; a content update.
research_provenance.motivating_incident: The product became usable (notes+chat, terminal/model launch, diagnostics, visual debugger, safe navigation via MT-097..103); a future no-context model needs the built-in manual updated to operate it — and the manual must be HONEST about which features landed a typed blocker.
research_provenance.validation_plan: the seven new no-context topics render + are selectable + the agent-tool id-audit keeps every author_id live + every mcp_tool real + the terminal/model topics state the honest typed-blocker condition + diagnostics/visual-debugger point to Settings; clippy -D warnings; full suite -j 2.
